Class AbstractToolResponse<C extends AbstractToolCall<?,?>>

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.agi.event.BasicPropertyChangeSource
uno.anahata.asi.agi.tool.spi.AbstractToolResponse<C>
Type Parameters:
C - The specific type of the Call this response is for.
All Implemented Interfaces:
PropertyChangeSource, Rebindable
Direct Known Subclasses:
BadToolResponse, JavaMethodToolResponse
public abstract class AbstractToolResponse<C extends AbstractToolCall<?,?>> extends BasicPropertyChangeSource
Represents the response of a tool call, designed for deferred execution. The fields are populated by the execute() method.

In the V2 architecture, the response is a logical state attribute of the AbstractToolCall rather than a top-level message part. It no longer inherits from AbstractPart and does not participate in history or pruning directly.

Author:
anahata-gemini-pro-2.5

    attachments
    A list of attachments (e.g., images, files) generated by the tool.

    The originating tool call.
    private String
    errors
    A descriptive error message if the tool failed or was rejected.
    private long
    executionTimeMillis
    The time taken to execute the method, in milliseconds.
    private boolean
    expanded
    Persistent UI state indicating if this response's panel is expanded in the conversation view.

    logs
    A list of log messages captured during the tool's execution.

    modifiedArgs
    Map of arguments that were modified by the user before execution.

    result
    The result of the invocation if it succeeded, otherwise null.

    status
    The final status of the invocation after execution.
    private Thread
    thread
    The physical executing Thread instance, marked transient to prevent Kryo serialization.
    private String
    threadName
    The name of the thread that executed this tool.
    private Integer
    tokenCount
    The cached token count of this response under the currently selected model.
    private String
    userFeedback
    Optional feedback from the user if the tool execution was prompted.

  • Field Details

    • call

      private final C extends AbstractToolCall<?,?> call
      The originating tool call.

    • status

      private ToolExecutionStatus status
      The final status of the invocation after execution.

    • result

      private Object result
      The result of the invocation if it succeeded, otherwise null.

    • errors

      private String errors
      A descriptive error message if the tool failed or was rejected.

    • executionTimeMillis

      private long executionTimeMillis
      The time taken to execute the method, in milliseconds.

    • threadName

      private String threadName
      The name of the thread that executed this tool.

    • thread

      private transient Thread thread
      The physical executing Thread instance, marked transient to prevent Kryo serialization.

    • logs

      private final List<String> logs
      A list of log messages captured during the tool's execution.

    • attachments

      private final List<ToolResponseAttachment> attachments
      A list of attachments (e.g., images, files) generated by the tool.

    • userFeedback

      private String userFeedback
      Optional feedback from the user if the tool execution was prompted.

    • modifiedArgs

      private final Map<String,Object> modifiedArgs
      Map of arguments that were modified by the user before execution. This is a snapshot of the modifications at the time of execution.

    • tokenCount

      private Integer tokenCount
      The cached token count of this response under the currently selected model. A value of null indicates that the token cost needs to be lazily recalculated.

    • expanded

      private boolean expanded
      Persistent UI state indicating if this response's panel is expanded in the conversation view.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractToolResponse

      public AbstractToolResponse(C call)
      Constructs a new AbstractToolResponse.
      Parameters:
      call - The originating tool call.

  • Method Details

    • setStatus

      public void setStatus(ToolExecutionStatus status)
      Sets the status of the tool execution and fires a property change event. Also updates the token count and checks if the tool prompt is complete.

      Metabolic Optimization: We only call autoSave() when reaching a terminal state (not PENDING or EXECUTING). This ensures transactional saves without redundant disk IO during tool conception or activation.

      Parameters:
      status - The new status.

    • setResult

      public void setResult(Object result)
      Sets the result of the tool execution and updates the token count.
      Parameters:
      result - The result object.

    • setErrors

      public void setErrors(String errors)
      Sets the error message and updates the token count.
      Parameters:
      errors - The error message.

    • setExpanded

      public void setExpanded(boolean expanded)
      Sets the persistent UI state for the response expansion and fires a property change event.
      Parameters:
      expanded - The new expanded state.

    • updateTokenCount

      private void updateTokenCount()
      Updates the estimated token count based on the current state of the response.

    • getTokenCount

      public int getTokenCount()
      Retrieves the cached token count for this response. If the value has not yet been calculated (is null), it triggers an on-the-fly calculation using the active model's tokenizer.
      Returns:
      The calculated token count, or 0 if no model is active.

    • calculateTokenCount

      private void calculateTokenCount()
      Performs the lazy, model-specific token calculation for this response.

    • resetTokenCount

      public void resetTokenCount()
      Resets the cached token count, forcing a lazy, background recalculation on the next call to getTokenCount().

    • getToolName

      public String getToolName()
      Gets the name of the tool that was invoked.
      Returns:
      The tool's name.

    • execute

      public abstract void execute()
      Executes the tool logic. This method is responsible for populating the status, result, errors, and executionTimeMillis fields.

    • stop

      public abstract void stop()
      Stops the tool execution if it is currently running.

    • fail

      public void fail(String reason)
      Rejects the tool call before execution, setting the status to FAILED.
      Parameters:
      reason - The reason for the rejection.

    • fail

      public void fail(String reason, String details)
      Rejects the tool call before execution with detailed context.
      Parameters:
      reason - The high-level reason for rejection.
      details - Additional technical details (e.g., a diff of the failed intent).

    • setThread

      public void setThread(Thread thread)
      Sets the running thread and updates the thread name.
      Parameters:
      thread - the executing thread.

    • decline

      public void decline()
      Resets the response to its initial state, clearing results, errors, logs, and attachments.

    • addLog

      public void addLog(String message)
      Adds a log message to this response, which can be displayed to the user.
      Parameters:
      message - The log message.

    • addError

      public void addError(Throwable t)
      Converts a exception to a string and adds it to the errors field.
      Parameters:
      t - The exception to add.

    • addError

      public void addError(String error)
      Adds a log message to this response, which can be displayed to the user.
      Parameters:
      error - The error message to add.

    • addAttachment

      public void addAttachment(byte[] data, String mimeType)
      Attaches a binary blob to this response.
      Parameters:
      data - The binary data.
      mimeType - The MIME type of the data.

    • removeAttachment

      public void removeAttachment(ToolResponseAttachment attachment)
      Removes an attachment from this response.
      Parameters:
      attachment - The attachment to remove.

    • getAttachments

      public List<ToolResponseAttachment> getAttachments()
      Gets an unmodifiable view of the attachments.
      Returns:
      The list of attachments.

    • clearLogs

      public void clearLogs()
      Clears any log messages captured during execution.

    • removeLogsIf

      public void removeLogsIf(Predicate<String> filter)
      Removes logs that match the given predicate, updates the token count, and fires a single property change event.
      Parameters:
      filter - The predicate to apply to each log message.

    • getExecutedArgs

      public Map<String,Object> getExecutedArgs()
      Gets the effective arguments that were used for execution. This merges the original immutable call arguments with the snapshot of modifications taken at execution time.
      Returns:
      A map of executed arguments.

    • getAgi

      public Agi getAgi()
      Gets the parent agi session.
      Returns:
      The agi session, or null if not attached.

    • asText

      public String asText()
      Returns the content of the response as a simple string.
      Returns:
      The text representation of the response.

    • rebind

      public void rebind()
      Re-initializes the transient PropertyChangeSupport after deserialization.
      Specified by:
      rebind in interface Rebindable
      Overrides:
      rebind in class BasicPropertyChangeSource
